briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to today's climate briefing on July 7, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis: Global Impacts and Warnings
sections.content: Today's news clearly illustrates the severity of the climate crisis facing the world and the Korean Peninsula. In Europe, despite efforts to reduce fine dust, the 'Eco-Syndemic' paradox has emerged, predicting an immense heatwave in 2026 [17]. In the UK, a June heatwave led to widespread sleep deprivation, with 86% of homes reported as 'too hot' [140]. The Korean Peninsula is also on the front lines of the climate crisis with extended summers and frequent heatwaves [16], while a super-sized marine heatwave in the Pacific is expected to intensify typhoons and heat domes [27]. Such extreme climate change is having widespread impacts, including pushing the 'Swallowtail butterfly,' which has endured for 200,000 years, to the brink of extinction [6], potentially causing billions of dollars in losses to Hawaii's coral reef tourism [127], reshaping the global fruit and vegetable map, and inflicting immense damage on roads and highways [129, 123, 130].

sections.title: 🌱 Climate Adaptation Efforts and Resilience Building
sections.content: Amidst this crisis, active efforts are underway to adapt to climate change and enhance resilience. Gyeongsangnam-do has been selected the most nationwide for 'Climate Change Response Pilot Aquaculture' projects, thereby increasing the climate adaptability of its future fisheries [1, 8]. Jeollanam-do is accelerating the 'constitutional improvement' of fruit and horticultural crop varieties in response to climate change [3], and the President of the Agricultural Economics Society emphasized that the climate crisis poses the greatest threat to agriculture, stressing the urgency of climate-responsive agriculture [32]. Furthermore, Yeoju City Public Health Center is providing 'climate welfare' to climate-vulnerable groups, stepping up to protect the socially disadvantaged [9]. Osan City is engaging citizens in 'drawing our neighborhood heat maps' to build data for heatwave response [20, 28, 29, 31]. These initiatives are part of multifaceted efforts to explore new ideas for cities in the era of climate crisis [7] and to address climate change and ocean issues through science, policy, education, and art, as seen in the '2026 OTIF Festival' [4].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating the Transition to a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: Various efforts are also continuing towards achieving a carbon-neutral society. The 2026 Climate Citizens' Assembly officially launched bottom-up governance by confirming three major climate agendas directly selected by citizens [10, 37, 69]. Yeongam-gun is strengthening climate crisis response education by operating a 'Carbon Neutral Agriculture School' to lead future generations in carbon-neutral practices [38, 45, 47, 58, 61]. Daejeon Daedeok-gu is raising citizen awareness by training 'Carbon Neutral Picture Book Instructors' [21, 39, 62, 76, 78, 82]. Gurye-gun is encouraging eco-friendly activities by introducing Jeonnam-type carbon-neutral platforms like 'Carbon Moa Tantane' [42, 46, 48, 55, 67, 79], and Jeju-do is accelerating the establishment of participatory carbon-neutral virtuous cycle models across the local community, including implementing a 'citizen daily reduction activity carbon credit' model [41, 51]. Corporations are also participating in spreading a culture of carbon-neutral practices through ESG management, such as HDC Labs' eco-friendly campaign [52, 75, 87] and Coway's concretization of its 2050 net-zero roadmap [117].

sections.title: 📰 Challenges and Tasks for Renewable Energy Expansion
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is a core task for achieving carbon neutrality. Yesan-gun is supporting the installation of new and renewable energy facilities in 50 households, reducing electricity bill burdens and contributing to greenhouse gas reduction [90, 95, 96, 99, 100, 103, 104, 107, 109, 111, 113, 114, 115, 116, 118, 119, 120]. The scope of recognized hydro-thermal energy has been significantly expanded, opening the way to utilize leaked groundwater and sewage as renewable energy [89]. POSCO recorded its highest level last year, increasing renewable energy use by 60% through solar and small hydropower generation [106]. Hanwha Q Cells secured a solar power project to supply electricity to Meta in the US [105], among other positive developments. However, many challenges remain in expanding renewable energy. Controversies over the setback distances for renewable energy facilities raise concerns about undermining protection measures for rural residents [92, 102]. Discrepancies between government policies are also being pointed out, such as difficulties faced by agricultural solar power projects due to green belt regulations [110]. Furthermore, the necessity for changes in grid technology to expand renewable energy [88] and the 'growing pains' of increasing carbon emissions despite increased renewable energy use [98] indicate the need for continuous attention and resolution efforts.

sections.title: ⚠️ The AI Era: Accuracy of Climate Information and the Energy Dilemma
sections.content: Artificial intelligence (AI) holds the potential to transform the paradigm of disaster safety research in the era of climate crisis [18], but simultaneously raises serious concerns. Significant factual distortions regarding climate change are occurring within AI systems, leading to calls for the establishment of transparency frameworks to verify this information [2]. Moreover, AI's enormous energy consumption is a major concern for sustainability leaders [139]. This, coupled with Europe's 'climate dilemma' between carbon neutrality and increased air conditioner use [86], suggests the importance of finding a balance between technological advancement and environmental protection. In this context, research indicating that citizens tend to underestimate or overestimate public participation in climate action [125] further highlights the importance of accurate and reliable climate information.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has highlighted the ongoing efforts in adaptation and mitigation amidst the severity of the climate crisis. As opportunities and challenges coexist, particularly in the expansion of renewable energy and the utilization of AI technology, sustained attention and wise responses are imperative. This concludes today's climate briefing.
